rulings.Case Title: BENJAMIN SALTHOUSE VS. KAUSHIK DATTANI et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, December 1, 2014, line 2. Defendant Maverick Restaurant Group Motion to Strike Third Amended Complaint is Continued to December 31, 2014. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C and provide courtesy copy of the operative complaint to department 501 no later than December 5, 2014 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date.

rulings.Case Title: DERICK DENIES VS. GEORGE HONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: No appearances, The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows DEFENDANT GEORGE HONG'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS OFF CALENDAR, untimely per CCP 1005. Motion filed on November 20, 2014 and set for hearing December 1, 2014. Opposing party and its counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B in future. ==Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: R. Goulding; Dept.501; Not Reported

rulings.Case Title: STERLING HEATLEY, AN INDIVIDUAL VS. SF CORNERS LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion's Calendar for Monday December 1, 2014, line 4. DEFENDANTS SF CORNERS LLC and BAY CITIES FINANCIAL CORP.'S DEMURRERS' TO COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to the first cause of action for partition to join "all persons having or claiming interests of record or actually known to the plaintiff¿" CCP 872.510. Moving party and its counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. TEDRONNE BRESTON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion's Calendar for Monday December 1, 2014, line 7. PLAINTIFF JOHN STEWART COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. Moving party shifted the burden and opposing party failed to submit admissible evidence to create a triable issue of fact. It remains undisputed that defendant was dragging and choking Mr. Harrison. Also, defendant fails to indicate how cessation of the UD action will "eliminate the risk" of defendant dragging/hitting/choking other tenants. Radecki v. Joura (1997) 114 F.3d 115. =(501/REQ)

Housing Court Law and Motion's Calendar for Monday December 1, 2014, line 8. DEFENDANT OVEDA MORALES' Demurrer to Complaint SUSTAINED with leave to amend to reconcile allegations in paragraph 7(b) and 8(2). No opposition filed. =(501/REQ)
